nodes.title: Seconds to frames  (In the case of 24fps)
Prompt Relay Encode (Timeline)
widgets_values:
max_frames:
 - Enter the number of frames corresponding to the desired video length. For 24fps, refer to the quick reference table above.

fps:
 - If you change the fps, change this value accordingly.

Global Prompt:
 - The top of the node is the "Global Prompt" area, where you 
 enter prompts that you want to apply consistently to the 
 entire scene.

Timeline:
 - At the bottom of the node, you can change the prompts you
 want to apply along the timeline. Clicking "+Add" in the 
 bottom right of the node will make colored segments appear on 
 the timeline. Select each segment, then enter the prompts you 
 want to apply to each at the bottom. You can freely visually 
 change the length of the segments at both ends.
 This allows you to apply different prompts at specified time 
 intervals as you wish. To delete a segment, click "Delete" in
 the bottom right.
 The length of each segment should be specified appropriately 
 according to the input prompt. For example, if the action 
 instructed by the prompt clearly takes more than 5 seconds,
 but only a 2-second segment is provided, it is unlikely that
 the output will be generated as intended.

Model Area:
 - Enable the necessary nodes depending on the checkpoint 
 type. The nodes to enable are listed for each of the three 
 model types on the left.

Sage Attention & Torch Settings:
 - Enable Sage Attention if it is installed. This may 
 improve generation speed.
 If it is not installed, disable it as an error will occur.
 Torch settings are optional.

Final Upscale:
 -Ltx2.3 first generates an image at half the resolution 
 specified in the first pass,creating a rough outline of the
 animation. Then,in the next pass, it upscales the 
 resolution to twice its original size,improving the overall 
 quality. This final upscale is used for further upscaling.
 However, this may result in image degradation, etc.
 If you are using an Nvidia RTX GPU, use the RTX node.
 Otherwise, replace it with a general-purpose upscaler or 
 another node.

RIFE Frame Interpolation:
 - This node performs frame interpolation. Enable it if
 you wish to use it. In that case, enter the initial
 frame rate in "Base Frame Rate" on the lower side, and 
 the desired frame rate after frame interpolation in 
 "Target Frame Rate".
  If you do not wish to use it, please disable "RIFE~" and 
 enable "Don't Use RIFE".

Power LoRa Loader:
 - The LoRa selection here can be applied separately to the 
 initial pass and the upscale pass. Ltx2.3 is very sensitive 
 to LoRa type and weight,so the setup can be very difficult.
 You may need to experiment repeatedly.
 Since the initial pass and upscale have different roles, 
 you don't necessarily need to apply the same LoRa or the 
 same weight. We recommend experimenting with different 
 approaches.

KSampler Select:
 - Select the type of sampler you want to use.
 For upscaling, use the one with "cfg_pp" in its name.

Base Frame Rate & Target Frame Rate:
 - The base frame rates for Ltx2.3 are 24, 25, 48, and 50, 
 but the recommended values ​​may vary depending on the model 
 used. For the target frame rate, if you are performing 
 frame interpolation using the RIFE Frame Interpolation node
 at the end of generation, specify the target number of 
 frames you want to output. If you are not using RIFE, no 
 setting is required.

Width & Height:
 - 720p to 1080p seems to be an appropriate resolution, but
 please try different settings.

Length (seconds):
 - I prefer using seconds rather than frames, so I specified 
 it in seconds. Please enter the value of max_frames in the
 Prompt Relay node to match the length set here.

Multi Image Loader:
 - Specify the anchor image. Upload images using "Upload images." 
 The images will be applied in order, so you need to arrange them 
 on the video timeline in the order you want them to appear. You 
 cannot upload two images with the same name consecutively. 
 However, you can if you change the name. The "Ltx Sequencer" 
 section on the right will increase in conjunction with the number
 of images you upload.

LTX Sequencer:
 - Even if multiple of these nodes exist, inputting a value in one 
 place will apply the same value to all nodes. Therefore, 
 inputting into this node in the input area (which is not 
 connected to anything) will automatically input the value into 
 the nodes in the sampler area as well.
 The image number is linked to the number in the multi-image
 loader, so it sets how many seconds on the timeline and with what
 weight the image with that number will be applied.
 Generally, it's best to set the weight of the first and last
 images to "0.8 to 1.0" and the intermediate images to around 
 "0.5". However, it's a good idea to experiment with different 
 settings.

nodes.title: Resample Audio (2) or Force First Pass Audio (1)

inputs.text: A medium close-up shot features a Caucasian man with a closely shaven head and face, wearing a black baseball cap with "PNTR" in white letters on the front, and a dark grey t-shirt with "JUST DO IT" visible across his chest. A small black microphone is clipped to his shirt collar. He is positioned slightly to the left of the frame, looking intently downwards and to his right, his eyes focused off-camera. His facial expression is one of deep concentration, with his brow slightly furrowed. As he looks down, a quick sniff sound is heard, and then he speaks with a deep male voice and a slightly frustrated tone, saying, "I think it's so bad." The camera remains static throughout, maintaining a shallow depth of field, which keeps the man in sharp focus while the background is softly blurred, revealing a light-colored wall with white wooden shelving or trim, and a partially open white wooden door on the right. After a brief pause, another short, audible sniff is heard. The man then continues to speak, his voice maintaining the same quality, as he states, "So bad. So bad." He elaborates further, emphasizing his point with a final statement, "This got to be, it's got to be the worst tool I've ever seen."
